questions.question: Explain the concept of a drainage basin.
questions.answer: A drainage basin is the area drained by a river and its tributaries.

questions.question: Explain the role of water divides in drainage systems.
questions.answer: Water divides separate drainage basins and determine the direction of river flow.

questions.question: Explain the erosional activity of Himalayan rivers.
questions.answer: Himalayan rivers erode deeply in mountainous regions forming gorges and valleys.

questions.question: Explain why Himalayan rivers carry large amounts of silt.
questions.answer: Because they flow through young fold mountains and erode rocks continuously.

questions.question: Explain the formation of the Sundarban delta.
questions.answer: It is formed by the deposition of sediments by the Ganga and Brahmaputra rivers.

questions.question: Explain the braided channel of the Brahmaputra.
questions.answer: It forms due to heavy silt deposition and frequent channel shifting.

questions.question: Explain why peninsular rivers have smaller drainage basins.
questions.answer: Because they have shorter courses and originate from plateaus.

questions.question: Explain the significance of rift valley rivers.
questions.answer: They flow through valleys formed by tectonic faulting.

questions.question: Explain the economic importance of river valleys.
questions.answer: They provide fertile land, water for irrigation and support settlements.

questions.question: Explain the role of rivers in hydroelectric power generation.
questions.answer: Flowing river water is used to rotate turbines to generate electricity.

questions.question: Explain the role of lakes in regulating river flow.
questions.answer: Lakes store excess water during rains and release it slowly during dry periods.

questions.question: Explain how oxbow lakes are formed.
questions.answer: They form when river meanders are cut off from the main channel.

questions.question: Explain why river pollution affects human health.
questions.answer: Because polluted water spreads diseases and contaminates drinking water.

questions.question: Explain why rivers shift their courses frequently.
questions.answer: Because of heavy sediment deposition and erosion.

questions.question: Explain the role of rivers in transportation.
questions.answer: Rivers provide a natural route for transporting goods and people.

questions.question: Explain the significance of river basins for agriculture.
questions.answer: River basins provide fertile soil and water for irrigation.

questions.question: Explain how lagoons are formed.
questions.answer: They are formed when sand bars separate part of the sea from the mainland.

questions.question: Explain why glaciers form many lakes in the Himalayan region.
questions.answer: Because melting glaciers fill depressions formed by glacial erosion.

questions.question: Explain the importance of river conservation.
questions.answer: It ensures sustainable use of freshwater resources.

questions.question: Explain why water resources must be managed carefully.
questions.answer: Because freshwater is limited and essential for life.

questions.question: Explain the role of rivers in settlement development.
questions.answer: Settlements develop along rivers due to availability of water and fertile land.

questions.question: Explain why river water quality is declining.
questions.answer: Due to increasing urbanisation, industrialisation and sewage discharge.

questions.question: Explain why delta regions are densely populated.
questions.answer: Because they have fertile soil suitable for agriculture.

questions.question: Explain the importance of rivers in India's economy.
questions.answer: They support agriculture, transportation and power generation.

questions.question: Explain why clean rivers are essential for human life.
questions.answer: Because they provide safe drinking water and support ecosystems.
